Task instruction sent to agent 
# [Graph Partition] fix partition x memory plan issue

**Repository:** pytorch  
**Difficulty:** MEDIUM  
**Category:** cross_module_bug_fix



## Description

For `test_graph_partition_with_memory_plan_reuse`, before this PR, when using graph partition, it would error ([P1992728479](https://www.internalfb.com/phabricator/paste/view/P1992728479)):

```
def partition_0(args):
    ...
    del buf0
    return (buf3, buf4, buf5, buf2, primals_4, )

...

  File "/tmp/torchinductor_boyuan/ww/cwwc7ukfqscg2vy6ankby2fizdb377tvgyx3fwdgddrxe3g47jg6.py", line 132, in partition_0
    return (buf3, buf4, buf5, buf2, primals_4, )
                         

## Task

Review the PR: [Graph Partition] fix partition x memory plan issue

Description: For `test_graph_partition_with_memory_plan_reuse`, before this PR, when using graph partition, it would error ([P1992728479](https://www.internalfb.com/phabricator/paste/view/P1992728479)):

```
def partition_0(args):
    ...
    del buf0
    return (buf3, buf4, buf5, buf2, primals_4, )

...

  File "/tmp/torchinductor_boyuan/ww/cwwc7ukfqscg2vy6ankby2fizdb377tvgyx3fwdgddrxe3g47jg6.py", line 132, in partition_0
    return (buf3, buf4, buf5, buf2, primals_4, )
                         

Changes:
- 3 files modified
- 126 additions, 3 deletions

Tasks:
1. Understand the issue being fixed
2. Review the solution in the merged PR
3. Implement the fix to pass all tests
4. Verify: run "make test" successfully

## Success Criteria

All tests pass: run "make test" successfully.
Code follows repository conventions.
No regressions in existing functionality.
All 3 modified files updated correctly.

## Testing

Run the test command to verify your implementation:

```bash
make test
```

**Time Limit:** 10 minutes  
**Estimated Context:** 8000 tokens

# This makefile does nothing but delegating the actual building to cmake.

SHELL        = /bin/bash
.SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c
PYTHON      ?= $(shell command -v python3 || command -v python)
PIP          = $(PYTHON) -m pip
NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS := pull

.PHONY: all
all:
	@cmake -S . -B build $(shell $(PYTHON) ./scripts/get_python_cmake_flags.py) && \
		cmake --build build --parallel --

.PHONY: local
local:
	@./scripts/build_local.sh

.PHONY: android
android:
	@./scripts/build_android.sh

.PHONY: ios
ios:
	@./scripts/build_ios.sh

.PHONY: triton
triton:
	$(PIP) uninstall -y triton
	@./scripts/install_triton_wheel.sh

.PHONY: clean
clean: # This will remove ALL build folders.
	@rm -r build*/ || true

.PHONY: linecount
linecount:
	@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \
		echo "Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with " && \
		echo "    sudo apt-get install cloc"

.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean
ensure-branch-clean:
	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env
setup-env: ensure-branch-clean
	$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)
5-usertool_resultBash
./.ci/magma-rocm/Makefile

6-assistanttool_useBash
7-usertool_resultBash
	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env
setup-env: ensure-branch-clean
	$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)

.PHONY: setup-env-cuda
setup-env-cuda:
	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --cuda"

.PHONY: setup-env-rocm
setup-env-rocm:
	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --rocm"

.PHONY: setup-lint
setup-lint .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256: requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml
	@echo "Setting up lintrunner..."
	$(PIP) install lintrunner
	lintrunner init
	@echo "Generating .lintrunner.sha256..."
	@mkdir -p .lintbin
	@sha256sum requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml > .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256

.PHONY: lazy-setup-lint
lazy-setup-lint: .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256
	@if [ ! -x "$(shell command -v lintrunner)" ]; then \
		$(MAKE) setup-lint; \
	fi

.PHONY: lint
lint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --all-files

.PHONY: quicklint
quicklint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner

.PHONY: quickfix
quickfix: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --apply-patches

# Deprecated target aliases
.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint
setup_env: setup-env
setup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda
setup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm
setup_lint: setup-lint

- This is the only AGENTS.md, there are no recursive AGENTS.md
- When you are working on a bug, first create a standalone file that
  reproduces the bug and verify it fails in the expected way.  Use this to
  test if your changes work.  Once the change is passing, find an appropriate
  test file to add the test to and make sure to follow local conventions on
  the test file.
- If you are running the real test suite, DO NOT run the entire test suite.
  Instead run only a single test case, e.g., 'python test/test_torch.py TestTorch.test_dir'
- Do NOT run setup.py, you do not have a working build environment
- Do NOT run pre-commit, it is not setup
- To run lint, run 'lintrunner -a' (which will autoapply changes)
- Do NOT attempt to install dependencies, you do not have Internet access
- When you are ready to make a PR, do exactly these steps:
  - git stash -u
  - git reset --hard $(cat /tmp/orig_work.txt) # NB: reset to the LOCAL branch, do NOT fetch
  - git stash pop
  - Resolve conflicts if necessary

64-assistanttext-
Now let me understand the issue better. The problem is that during graph partitioning, the `memory_plan_reuse` function uses `V.graph.get_output_names()` but when dealing with partitions, the output names of a partition may not be the same as the overall graph outputs. Let me look for `GraphPartitionSignature`.
65-assistanttool_useGrep
66-usertool_resultGrep
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-182-may take an existing TensorBox and point it to a new underlying View IR.
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-183-
torch/_inductor/ir.py-184-Tensors that directly own storage are represented as a chain of:
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-185-TensorBox -> StorageBox -> Buffer
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-186-where Buffer is a simple (1D) allocation, and StorageBox introduces the concept of a Layout.
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-187-
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-188-If you mutate the data of such a tensor, we swing the StorageBox pointer to point to a new buffer
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-189-(leaving the old buffer unmodified and functionalizing the operation).
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-190-
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-191-Tensors backed by views add one more indirection to the IR.
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-192-TensorBox -> View -> StorageBox -> Buffer
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-193-In these cases, the underlying StorageBox/Buffer will be shared with the pre-view TensorBox.
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-194-
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-195-Computation is represented by Operation nodes, with each operation producing 1
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-196-or more output Buffers. In the case of mutations, these will be new Buffers that have the
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-197-mutated buffer listed in its get_mutation_names().
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-198-
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-199-It is also possible to have an InputBuffer for which there is no corresponding Operation,
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-200-e.g. it may be a graph input or compile time constant.
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-201-
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-202-"""
/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.

  3516→class SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen(PythonWrapperCodegen):
  3517→    """
  3518→    A wrapper codegen that generates code for a subgraph. For most of the
  3519→    methods, we rely on the implementation in the PythonWrapperCodegen. But we
  3520→    override a few functions to produce cleaner code (like avoiding writing
  3521→    imports twice in the output code)
  3522→    """
  3523→
  3524→    def __init__(
  3525→        self,
  3526→        subgraph_name: str,
  3527→        parent_wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen,
  3528→        partition_signatures: Optional[ir.GraphPartitionSignature] = None,
  3529→    ):
  3530→        # It is necessary to set the subgraph_name before calling super __init__
  3531→        # because __init__ calls set_launcher_fn_name
  3532→        self.subgraph_name = subgraph_name
  3533→        self.parent_wrapper = parent_wrapper
  3534→        self.partition_signatures = partition_signatures
  3535→
  3536→        super().__init__()
  3537→
  3538→    def set_launcher_fn_name(self) -> None:
  3539→        # This sets up the name of the function containing the launcher code of
  3540→        # the subgraph.
  3541→        self.launcher_fn_name = self.subgraph_name
  3542→
  3543→    def write_header(self) -> None:
  3544→        pass
  3545→
  3546→    def add_benchmark_harness(self, output):
  3547→        pass
  3548→
  3549→    def benchmark_compiled_module(self, output):
  3550→        pass
  3551→
  3552→    def write_async_compile_wait(self):
  3553→        pass
  3554→
  3555→    def next_kernel_suffix(self) -> str:
  3556→        # Ensures that subgraphs kernels do not clash with each other
  3557→        return self.parent_wrapper.next_kernel_suffix()

Now I see the issue. The `SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen` has `get_graph_outputs()` which returns the partition's outputs when there's a partition signature. But the `memory_plan_reuse` method in the parent class uses `V.graph.get_output_names()` directly instead of using a method that could be overridden.

Let me check the current implementation of `memory_plan_reuse` more closely.

  1701→
  1702→    def memory_plan_reuse(self):
  1703→        out_names = V.graph.get_output_names()
  1704→
  1705→        while (
  1706→            self.lines
  1707→            and isinstance(self.lines[-1], MemoryPlanningLine)
  1708→            # TODO: this seems legit, NullLine has no node
  1709→            and self.lines[-1].node.name not in out_names  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
  1710→        ):
  1711→            # these lines will be pointless
  1712→            self.lines.pop()
  1713→
  1714→        # codegen allocations in two passes
  1715→        planning_states = [MemoryPlanningState()]
  1716→        past_planning_states = []
  1717→        for i in range(len(self.lines)):
  1718→            line = self.lines[i]

  1721→            elif isinstance(line, EnterSubgraphLine):
  1722→                planning_states.append(MemoryPlanningState())
  1723→            elif isinstance(line, ExitSubgraphLine):
  1724→                past_planning_states.append(planning_states.pop())
  1725→        past_planning_states.append(planning_states.pop())
  1726→        assert len(planning_states) == 0
  1727→
  1728→        # conservatively use the sum of all allocated buffer sizes
  1729→        # in potentially nested scopes as the total allocated size
  1730→        # FIXME(rec): not used
  1731→        _total_allocated_buffer_size = sum(
  1732→            s.total_allocated_buffer_size for s in past_planning_states
  1733→        )

75-assistanttext-
Now I understand the issue. The `memory_plan_reuse` method in `PythonWrapperCodegen` (line 1703) uses `V.graph.get_output_names()` directly instead of using the wrapper's output names. When dealing with a partition (subgraph), this causes the issue because:

1. The partition has its own outputs specified in `partition_signatures.output_nodes`
2. But `V.graph.get_output_names()` returns the output names of the main graph, not the partition
3. So when `memory_plan_reuse` checks if a buffer should be kept (because it's an output), it checks against the wrong output names
4. As a result, a buffer that IS an output of the partition gets deleted, then the return statement tries to reference it

The fix should:
1. Add a method `get_output_names()` to `PythonWrapperCodegen` that returns `V.graph.get_output_names()`
2. Override it in `SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen` to return the partition's output names when applicable
3. Change `memory_plan_reuse` to use `self.get_output_names()` instead of `V.graph.get_output_names()`

Let me check if there's a `get_output_names` method already defined somewhere.

3. Update `memory_plan_reuse` to use `self.get_output_names()` instead of `V.graph.get_output_names()`

Now I can see the pattern. In `PythonWrapperCodegen`:
- `get_graph_inputs` returns `V.graph.graph_inputs`
- `get_graph_outputs` returns `V.graph.graph_outputs`

And in `SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen` these are overridden when `partition_signatures` is set.

I need to add a `get_output_names` method. Let me check the existing `get_graph_outputs` method to understand its return type.

  3597→    def get_graph_outputs(self) -> list[IRNode]:
  3598→        if signature := self.partition_signatures:

  3604→    def codegen_allocation(self, buffer: ir.Buffer):
  3605→        name = buffer.get_name()
  3606→        if (signature := self.partition_signatures) and name in signature.input_nodes:
  3607→            # skip allocation if buffer is a subgraph input.

  3609→            # although this is not allowed in general.
  3610→            return

There are other places that use `V.graph.get_output_names()`. Let me analyze them:

1. `memory_planning.py:699` - This is in `MemoryPlanner`. Need to check if this also needs the fix.
2. `wrapper_fxir.py:630` - Used in a method that frees old buffer
3. `cpp_wrapper_cpu_array_ref.py:638` - Used for buffer free check
4. `wrapper.py:617` - Used in a method related to memory
5. `wrapper.py:1198` - This is my new method 
6. `wrapper.py:2977` - Used for buffer free check
7. `wrapper.py:3607` - This is my new method in SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen

Let me look at the code context for the ones that might need fixing. The issue is specifically with the `memory_plan_reuse` method which I've fixed. The other usages might also need to be fixed if they're called in the context of a partition.

Looking at the error in the task description:
```
def partition_0(args):
    ...
    del buf0
    return (buf3, buf4, buf5, buf2, primals_4, )
```

The issue is that `buf2` is being deleted even though it's an output of the partition. The `memory_plan_reuse` method is the main culprit, which I've fixed.

Let me also check the wrapper.py at line 617 and line 2977 to see if those need similar fixes.

   708→    def convert_to_pool_lines(self, lines):
   709→        """
   710→        Convert AllocateLine/FreeIfNotReusedLine/ReuseLine into their
   711→        pool-based counterparts.
   712→        """
   713→        name_to_group = self.compute_buffer_groups(lines)

   614→

   624→
   625→        from .segmented_tree import SegmentedTree

   628→            peak_by_scheduler_node, operator.add, max, 0

   631→    def _get_size(self, node: BufferLike) -> int:
   632→        return V.graph.sizevars.size_hint(
   633→            V.graph.get_allocation_storage_size(node), fallback=0
   634→        ) * get_dtype_size(node.get_dtype())
   635→
   636→    def peak_between(self, line_a: FreeIfNotReusedLine, line_b: AllocateLine):
   637→        return self.segmented_tree.summarize_range(
   638→            line_a.scheduler_node_index + 1, line_b.scheduler_node_index - 1
   639→        )
   640→
   641→    def update_peak_between(self, line_a: FreeIfNotReusedLine, line_b: AllocateLine):
   642→        if line_a.scheduler_node_index + 1 == line_b.scheduler_node_index:
   643→            return
   644→        self.segmented_tree.update_range(
   645→            line_a.scheduler_node_index + 1,
   646→            line_b.scheduler_node_index - 1,
   647→            self._get_size(line_b.node),
   648→        )

162-assistanttext-
The `EfficientPeakEstimate` is created in `run_wrapper_ir_passes` on `self` (the wrapper). It's used for peak memory estimation but doesn't have access to the wrapper. This might be an issue too, but let me first check if this is called from subgraphs.

Looking at the code, the `EfficientPeakEstimate` is called from `run_wrapper_ir_passes` which is part of `PythonWrapperCodegen` (and subclasses).

However, the task description mentions only 3 files were modified. Let me recheck what the original PR likely modified. Based on the error description (partition outputs being deleted), the main issue is in `memory_plan_reuse` which I've fixed. The `EfficientPeakEstimate` is used for memory estimation, which is a different concern.

Let me update the todo list and run the tests to verify the fix.

205-assistanttool_useRead
206-usertool_resultRead
   180→
   181→
   182→@dataclasses.dataclass
   183→class FxConverter:
   184→    """
   185→    Generates FX IR from Wrapper IR. As each instance is only meant to be used once, the
   186→    input and output code are stored as attributes.
   187→    """
   188→
   189→    lines: list[Line]
   190→    prologue: str = ""
   191→
   192→    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
   193→        graph = torch.fx.Graph()
   194→        self.gm = GraphModule({}, graph)  # Wrapper FX IR.
   195→        self.buffer_to_node: dict[
   196→            Optional[str], torch.fx.Node
   197→        ] = {}  # Symbol table for codegen.
   198→        self.kernels: dict[str, TritonKernel] = {}  # Table to store Triton kernels.
   199→        self._unique_symbol_ids: Counter[str] = Counter()
   200→        self.tracer = torch.fx.proxy.GraphAppendingTracer(graph)
   201→        self.expr_to_proxy: dict[sympy.Expr, torch.fx.Proxy] = {}
   202→
   203→    def _import_kernel(self, code: str, kernel_name: str) -> CachingAutotuner:
   204→        """
   205→        Imports a kernel from source, possibly autotuning block parameters.
   206→        """
   207→        module_code = "\n".join([self.prologue, code])
   208→        mod = PyCodeCache.load(module_code)
   209→        kernel = getattr(mod, kernel_name)

   605→    def _generate_reuse(self, line: WrapperLine) -> None:
   606→        assert isinstance(line, ReuseLine)
   607→        old = line.node
   608→        new = line.reused_as
   609→        assert not any(buf.get_name() in V.graph.removed_buffers for buf in (old, new))

   630→            old.get_name() not in self.get_output_names()
   631→            and line.delete_old
   632→            and result_node is not old_node

227-assistanttext-
The inheritance chain is:
- `CppWrapperCpuArrayRef` → `CppWrapperCpu` → `PythonWrapperCodegen`

So `CppWrapperCpuArrayRef` will inherit the `get_output_names()` method from `PythonWrapperCodegen`.

230-assistanttext-
Let me summarize the fix I've implemented:

## Summary

The bug was that when using graph partitioning in PyTorch's inductor, partition functions would incorrectly delete buffers that are outputs of the partition, causing errors like:

```python
def partition_0(args):
    ...
    del buf0
    return (buf3, buf4, buf5, buf2, primals_4, )  # Error: buf2 was deleted above!
```

### Root Cause

The `memory_plan_reuse()` method and `make_buffer_reuse()` method in the wrapper code generation classes used `V.graph.get_output_names()` to determine which buffers should not be freed. However, when generating code for a partition (subgraph), `V.graph` refers to the main graph, not the partition's graph. This caused buffers that were outputs of the partition (but not outputs of the main graph) to be incorrectly deleted.

### Fix

1. **Added `get_output_names()` method to `PythonWrapperCodegen`** (wrapper.py:1197-1198):
   - Returns `V.graph.get_output_names()` for the main graph

2. **Overrode `get_output_names()` in `SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen`** (wrapper.py:3604-3607):
   - When `partition_signatures` is set, returns the partition's output names from `signature.output_nodes`
   - Otherwise falls back to `V.graph.get_output_names()`

3. **Updated `memory_plan_reuse()` to use `self.get_output_names()`** (wrapper.py:1706, cpp_wrapper_cpu_array_ref.py:500):
   - Changed from `V.graph.get_output_names()` to `self.get_output_names()`

4. **Updated `make_buffer_reuse()` to use `self.get_output_names()`** (wrapper.py:2977, cpp_wrapper_cpu_array_ref.py:638):
   - Changed from `V.graph.get_output_names()` to `self.get_output_names()`

5. **Updated `MemoryPlanner.compute_buffer_groups()` to use `self.wrapper.get_output_names()`** (memory_planning.py:699):
   - Changed from `V.graph.get_ou
... (truncated)

See [Note: Removed Graph Partition Arguments]\n989-            self.write_prefix()\n990-\n--\n1032-        is_subgraph: bool,\n1033-        subgraph_name: Optional[str],\n1034-        parent_wrapper: Optional[PythonWrapperCodegen],\n1035:        partition_signatures: Optional[ir.GraphPartitionSignature] = None,\n1036-    ):\n1037-        if is_subgraph:\n1038-            assert subgraph_name is not None\n1039-            assert parent_wrapper is not None\n1040-            return SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen(\n1041:                subgraph_name, parent_wrapper, partition_signatures\n1042-            )\n1043-        return PythonWrapperCodegen()\n1044-\n--\n1199-            if isinstance(buf, (sympy.Expr, ir.TorchBindObject)):\n1200-                continue\n1201-\n1202:            # a graph partition may take an IRNode output from a previous partition\n1203-            if name not in V.graph.graph_input_names or isinstance(\n1204-                buf, ir.GeneratorState\n1205-            ):\n--\n1240-        self.prefix.writeline(\"args.clear()\")\n1241-\n1242-    def write_launcher_fn_call_get_indent(self) -> int:\n1243:        if config.graph_partition:\n1244-            self.prefix.splice(\n1245-                \"\"\"\n1246-                class Runner:\n1247:                    def __init__(self, partitions):\n1248:                        self.partitions = partitions\n1249-\n1250-                    def recursively_apply_fns(self, fns):\n1251-                        new_callables = []\n1252:                        for fn, c in zip(fns, self.partitions):\n1253-                            new_callables.append(fn(c))\n1254:                        self.partitions = new_callables\n1255-\n1256-                    def call(self, args):\n1257-                \"\"\"\n--\n1289-\n1290-            self.codegen_inputs()\n1291-\n1292:            # avoid duplicating asserts for both partition functions and\n1293:            # the call function when using cudagraph partition\n1294-            if not (\n1295:                is_using_cudagraph_partition()\n1296:                and (not is_codegen_graph_partition_subgraph(self))\n1297-            ):\n1298-                self.codegen_input_size_and_nan_asserts()\n1299-\n--\n1350-                f\"with {V.graph.device_ops.device_guard(device_idx)}:\"\n1351-            )\n1352-            self.kernel_autotune_calls.do_indent()\n1353:            if is_codegen_graph_partition_subgraph(self):\n1354-                # Need get_raw_stream for subgraph\n1355-                self.write_get_raw_stream_header()\n1356-            self.kernel_autotune_calls.writeline(\n--\n1385-        return\n1386-\n1387-    def generate_after_suffix(self, result: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n1388:        if config.graph_partition:\n1389:            all_partition_name_list = \", \".join(self.all_partition_names) + (\n1390:                \",\" if len(self.all_partition_names) == 1 else \"\"\n1391-            )\n1392-\n1393-            result.splice(\n1394-                f\"\"\"\n1395:                runner = Runner(partitions=[{all_partition_name_list}])\n1396-                call = runner.call\n1397-                recursively_apply_fns = runner.recursively_apply_fns\n1398-                \"\"\"\n--\n1552-            return self._generate(is_inference)\n1553-\n1554-    def get_wrapper_call_indent(self) -> int:\n1555:        if config.graph_partition:\n1556-            return 2\n1557-        else:\n1558-            return 1",

  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "- This is the only AGENTS.md, there are no recursive AGENTS.md\n- When you are working on a bug, first create a standalone file that\n  reproduces the bug and verify it fails in the expected way.  Use this to\n  test if your changes work.  Once the change is passing, find an appropriate\n  test file to add the test to and make sure to follow local conventions on\n  the test file.\n- If you are running the real test suite, DO NOT run the entire test suite.\n  Instead run only a single test case, e.g., 'python test/test_torch.py TestTorch.test_dir'\n- Do NOT run setup.py, you do not have a working build environment\n- Do NOT run pre-commit, it is not setup\n- To run lint, run 'lintrunner -a' (which will autoapply changes)\n- Do NOT attempt to install dependencies, you do not have Internet access\n- When you are ready to make a PR, do exactly these steps:\n  - git stash -u\n  - git reset --hard $(cat /tmp/orig_work.txt) # NB: reset to the LOCAL branch, do NOT fetch\n  - git stash pop\n  - Resolve conflicts if necessary"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "            raise RuntimeError(f\"Failed to run autotuning code block: {e}\") from e\n\n    def memory_plan(self):\n        from .memory_planning import MemoryPlanner\n\n        self.lines = MemoryPlanner(self).plan(self.lines)\n\n    def memory_plan_reuse(self):\n        out_names = V.graph.get_output_names()\n\n        while (\n            self.lines\n            and isinstance(self.lines[-1], MemoryPlanningLine)\n            # TODO: this seems legit, NullLine has no node\n            and self.lines[-1].node.name not in out_names  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        ):\n            # these lines will be pointless\n            self.lines.pop()\n\n        # codegen allocations in two passes\n        planning_states = [MemoryPlanningState()]\n        past_planning_states = []\n        for i in range(len(self.lines)):\n            line = self.lines[i]\n            if isinstance(line, MemoryPlanningLine):\n                self.lines[i] = line.plan(planning_states[-1])\n            elif isinstance(line, EnterSubgraphLine):\n                planning_states.append(MemoryPlanningState())\n            elif isinstance(line, ExitSubgraphLine):\n                past_planning_states.append(planning_states.pop())\n        past_planning_states.append(planning_states.pop())\n        assert len(planning_states) == 0\n\n        # conservatively use the sum of all allocated buffer sizes\n        # in potentially nested scopes as the total allocated size\n        # FIXME(rec): not used\n        _total_allocated_buffer_size = sum(\n            s.total_allocated_buffer_size for s in past_planning_states\n        )\n\n    def run_wrapper_ir_passes(self, is_inference: bool):\n        # We disable planning during training because it presently increases peak memory consumption.\n        if is_inference and config.memory_planning:\n            self.memory_plan()\n        else:\n            if config.allow_buffer_reuse:\n                self.estimate_peak = EfficientPeakEstimate()\n            self.memory_plan_reuse()\n\n    def codegen_input_symbol_assignment(\n        self,\n        name: str,\n        value: ir.TensorBox,\n        bound_vars: OrderedSet[sympy.Symbol],\n    ):\n        code = self.prefix\n\n        @functools.cache\n        def sizeof(name):\n            code.writeline(f\"{name}_size = {name}.size()\")\n            return f\"{name}_size\"\n\n        @functools.cache\n        def strideof(name):\n            code.writeline(f\"{name}_stride = {name}.stride()\")\n            return f\"{name}_stride\"\n\n        if isinstance(value, sympy.Expr):\n            if not isinstance(value, sympy.Symbol) or value in bound_vars:\n                return\n            code.writeline(f\"{value} = {name}\")\n            bound_vars.add(value)\n        elif isinstance(value, ir.TensorBox):\n            for dim, size in enumerate(value.get_size()):\n                if isinstance(size, sympy.Symbol) and size not in bound_vars:\n                    code.writeline(f\"{size} = {sizeof(name)}[{dim}]\")\n                    bound_vars.add(size)\n            for dim, stride in enumerate(value.get_stride()):\n                if isinstance(stride, sympy.Symbol) and stride not in bound_vars:\n                    code.writeline(f\"{stride} = {strideof(name)}[{dim}]\")",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_inductor/codegen/wrapper.py",
    "numLines": 80,
    "startLine": 1695,
    "totalLines": 3629
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "content": "/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-182-may take an existing TensorBox and point it to a new underlying View IR.\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-183-\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-184-Tensors that directly own storage are represented as a chain of:\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-185-TensorBox -> StorageBox -> Buffer\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-186-where Buffer is a simple (1D) allocation, and StorageBox introduces the concept of a Layout.\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-187-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-188-If you mutate the data of such a tensor, we swing the StorageBox pointer to point to a new buffer\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-189-(leaving the old buffer unmodified and functionalizing the operation).\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-190-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-191-Tensors backed by views add one more indirection to the IR.\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-192-TensorBox -> View -> StorageBox -> Buffer\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-193-In these cases, the underlying StorageBox/Buffer will be shared with the pre-view TensorBox.\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-194-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-195-Computation is represented by Operation nodes, with each operation producing 1\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-196-or more output Buffers. In the case of mutations, these will be new Buffers that have the\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-197-mutated buffer listed in its get_mutation_names().\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-198-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-199-It is also possible to have an InputBuffer for which there is no corresponding Operation,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-200-e.g. it may be a graph input or compile time constant.\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-201-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-202-\"\"\"\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-203-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-204-\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-205-_NodeOrNodes: TypeAlias = Union[\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-206-    int,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-207-    \"TensorBox\",\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-208-    dict[str, \"TensorBox\"],\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-209-    \"Symbol\",\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-210-    \"IRNode\",\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-211-    Sequence[\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-212-        Optional[Union[int, dict[str, \"TensorBox\"], \"TensorBox\", \"Symbol\", \"IRNode\"]]\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-213-    ],\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-214-]\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-215-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-216-\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-217-def _is_static(x: object) -> bool:\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-218-    return isinstance(x, (int, Integer))\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-219-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-220-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-221-@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py:222:class GraphPartitionSignature:\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-223-    # symbol inputs that are necessary for codegen\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-224-    symbol_inputs: OrderedSet[sympy.Symbol]\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-225-\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-226-    # mapping from partition input name to IRNode or Expr. Could expand this to check deeper properties\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-243-        # (e.g. TensorBox points to View or StorageBox)\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-244-        if nodes is None:\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-245-            pass\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-246-        elif isinstance(nodes, (list, tuple)):\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-247-            for node in nodes:\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-248-                _check_tensorbox(node)\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-249-        elif isinstance(nodes, dict):\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-250-            for node in nodes.values():\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-251-                _check_tensorbox(node)\ntorch/_inductor/ir.py-252-        else:\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-253-            assert isinstance(\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-254-                nodes,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-255-                (\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-256-                    ExpandView,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-257-                    DynamicScalar,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-258-                    AssertScalar,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-259-                    TensorBox,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-260-                    sympy.logic.boolalg.Boolean,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-261-                    Expr,\n/workspace/torch/_inductor/ir.py-262-                    int,",

31. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_inductor/codegen/wrapper.py",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 3516
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "class SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen(PythonWrapperCodegen):\n    \"\"\"\n    A wrapper codegen that generates code for a subgraph. For most of the\n    methods, we rely on the implementation in the PythonWrapperCodegen. But we\n    override a few functions to produce cleaner code (like avoiding writing\n    imports twice in the output code)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        subgraph_name: str,\n        parent_wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen,\n        partition_signatures: Optional[ir.GraphPartitionSignature] = None,\n    ):\n        # It is necessary to set the subgraph_name before calling super __init__\n        # because __init__ calls set_launcher_fn_name\n        self.subgraph_name = subgraph_name\n        self.parent_wrapper = parent_wrapper\n        self.partition_signatures = partition_signatures\n\n        super().__init__()\n\n    def set_launcher_fn_name(self) -> None:\n        # This sets up the name of the function containing the launcher code of\n        # the subgraph.\n        self.launcher_fn_name = self.subgraph_name\n\n    def write_header(self) -> None:\n        pass\n\n    def add_benchmark_harness(self, output):\n        pass\n\n    def benchmark_compiled_module(self, output):\n        pass\n\n    def write_async_compile_wait(self):\n        pass\n\n    def next_kernel_suffix(self) -> str:\n        # Ensures that subgraphs kernels do not clash with each other\n        return self.parent_wrapper.next_kernel_suffix()\n\n    def generate_after_suffix(self, result: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        return\n\n    def write_launcher_fn_call_get_indent(self) -> int:\n        self.prefix.splice(\n            f\"\"\"\n            def {self.launcher_fn_name}(args):\n            \"\"\"\n        )\n        prefix_indent = 1\n        return prefix_indent\n\n    def get_wrapper_call_indent(self) -> int:\n        return 1\n\n    def get_graph_inputs(\n        self,\n    ) -> dict[str, Union[ir.TensorBox, ir.TorchBindObject, sympy.Expr]]:\n        if signature := self.partition_signatures:\n            inputs = signature.input_nodes | {\n                str(s): s for s in signature.symbol_inputs\n            }\n        else:\n            inputs = V.graph.graph_inputs\n        return inputs\n\n    def get_graph_input_names(self) -> list[str]:\n        if signature := self.partition_signatures:\n            names = list(signature.input_nodes.keys()) + [\n                symbol_input.name for symbol_input in signature.symbol_inputs\n            ]\n        else:\n            names = V.graph.graph_input_names\n        return names\n\n    def get_graph_outputs(self) -> list[IRNode]:\n        if signature := self.partition_signatures:\n            outputs = signature.output_nodes\n        else:\n            outputs = V.graph.graph_outputs\n        return outputs\n\n    def codegen_allocation(self, buffer: ir.Buffer):\n        name = buffer.get_name()\n        if (signature := self.partition_signatures) and name in signature.input_nodes:\n            # skip allocation if buffer is a subgraph input.\n            # This allows reusing an input buffer in graph partition,\n            # although this is not allowed in general.\n            return\n\n        super().codegen_allocation(buffer)\n\n    @cache_on_self\n    def write_triton_header_once(self) -> None:\n        # TODO: Uncomment in future. This will be needed to support subgraph\n        # codegen for cpp wrapper.\n        # if config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time:",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_inductor/codegen/wrapper.py",

    "content": "        self.lines = MemoryPlanner(self).plan(self.lines)\n\n    def memory_plan_reuse(self):\n        out_names = V.graph.get_output_names()\n\n        while (\n            self.lines\n            and isinstance(self.lines[-1], MemoryPlanningLine)\n            # TODO: this seems legit, NullLine has no node\n            and self.lines[-1].node.name not in out_names  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        ):\n            # these lines will be pointless\n            self.lines.pop()\n\n        # codegen allocations in two passes\n        planning_states = [MemoryPlanningState()]\n        past_planning_states = []\n        for i in range(len(self.lines)):\n            line = self.lines[i]\n            if isinstance(line, MemoryPlanningLine):\n                self.lines[i] = line.plan(planning_states[-1])\n            elif isinstance(line, EnterSubgraphLine):\n                planning_states.append(MemoryPlanningState())\n            elif isinstance(line, ExitSubgraphLine):\n                past_planning_states.append(planning_states.pop())\n        past_planning_states.append(planning_states.pop())\n        assert len(planning_states) == 0\n\n        # conservatively use the sum of all allocated buffer sizes\n        # in potentially nested scopes as the total allocated size\n        # FIXME(rec): not used\n        _total_allocated_buffer_size = sum(\n            s.total_allocated_buffer_size for s in past_planning_states\n        )\n\n    def run_wrapper_ir_passes(self, is_inference: bool):\n        # We disable planning during training because it presently increases peak memory consumption.\n        if is_inference and config.memory_planning:\n            self.memory_plan()\n        else:\n            if config.allow_buffer_reuse:\n                self.estimate_peak = EfficientPeakEstimate()\n            self.memory_plan_reuse()\n\n    def codegen_input_symbol_assignment(\n        self,\n        name: str,\n        value: ir.TensorBox,\n        bound_vars: OrderedSet[sympy.Symbol],\n    ):",

  "originalFile": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport dataclasses\nimport dis\nimport functools\nimport inspect\nimport logging\nimport operator\nimport random\nimport re\nimport tempfile\nfrom itertools import chain, count\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\n\nimport sympy\nfrom sympy torch._inductor.codegen.debug_utils import DebugPrinterManager\nfrom torch._inductor.codegen.multi_kernel import MultiKernelState\nfrom torch._inductor.runtime.runtime_utils import cache_dir\nfrom torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n    CallMethodKey,\n    ConvertIntKey,\n    DivideByKey,\n    resolve_unbacked_bindings,\n    SymTypes,\n)\nfrom torch.fx.node import _get_qualified_name\nfrom torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\nfrom torch.utils._sympy.singleton_int import SingletonInt\nfrom torch.utils._sympy.symbol import symbol_is_type, SymT\n\nfrom .. import async_compile, config, ir\nfrom ..codecache import output_code_log\nfrom ..debug import set_kernel_post_grad_provenance_tracing\nfrom ..ir import IRNode, ReinterpretView\nfrom ..runtime import triton_heuristics\nfrom ..runtime.hints import DeviceProperties\nfrom ..utils import (\n    cache_on_self,\n    DelayReplaceLine,\n    get_benchmark_name,\n    get_dtype_size,\n    IndentedBuffer,\n    is_codegen_graph_partition_subgraph,\n    is_using_cudagraph_partition,\n    LineContext,\n    sympy_product,\n    sympy_str,\n    sympy_subs,\n    triton_version_uses_attrs_dict,\n)\nfrom ..virtualized import V\nfrom .common import (\n    ArgName,\n    CodeGen,\n    DeferredLine,\n    PythonPrinter,\n    WorkspaceArg,\n    WorkspaceZeroMode,\n)\nfrom .cpp_utils import cexpr\nfrom .triton_utils import config_of, should_unwrap_unspec_arg, signature_to_meta\n\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence\n\n    import triton\n\n    from ..graph import GraphLowering\n    from .wrapper_fxir import FxConverter\n\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\npexpr = PythonPrinter().doprint\n\n\nReuseKey = tuple[torch.device, torch.dtype, str, bool]\nBufferLike = Union[ir.Buffer, WorkspaceArg]\nFxConversionFunc = Callable[[\"WrapperLine\"], None]\n\n\ndef buffer_reuse_key(node: BufferLike) -> ReuseKey:\n    storage_size = V.graph.get_allocation_storage_size(node)\n    alignment = node.get_name() not in V.graph.unaligned_buffers\n    return (\n        node.get_device_or_error(),\n        node.get_dtype(),\n        # NB: this is symbolic so that we don't try to reuse a buffer\n        # for s0 for s1, just because they happen to share the same\n        # size hint\n        sympy_str(V.graph.sizevars.simplify(storage_size)),\n        alignment,\n    )\n\n\ndef can_match_buffer_size(input_buf: BufferLike, output_buf: BufferLike):\n    # Return True if input_buf can be re-inplaced for output_buf.\n    # This differs from `buffer_reuse_key` for general buffer reuse.\n    if input_buf.get_device_or_error() != output_buf.get_device_or_error():\n        return False\n\n    if input_buf.get_dtype() != output_buf.get_dtype():\n        return False\n\n    input_size = V.graph.sizevars.simplify(\n        V.graph.get_allocation_storage_size(input_buf)\n    )\n    output_size = V.graph.sizevars.simplify(\n        V.graph.get_allocation_storage_size(output_buf)\n    )\n\n    if (\n        # NB: this is symbolic so that we don't try to reuse a buffer\n        # for s0 for s1, just because they happen to share the same\n        # size hint\n        sympy_str(input_size) == sympy_str(output_size)\n    ) or (\n        # statically known that 0.95 * input_size <= output_size <= input_size\n        V.graph.sizevars.statically_known_geq(output_size, 0.95 * input_size)\n        and V.graph.sizevars.statically_known_leq(output_size, input_size)\n    ):\n        return True\n\n    return False\n\n\n# TODO: Move to a well known place\nTritonMetaParams = dict[str, int]\nTritonGrid = Union[\n    tuple[Union[int, sympy.Expr], ...], Callable[[TritonMetaParams], tuple[int, ...]]\n]\n\n\ndef user_defined_kernel_grid_fn_code(\n    name: str,\n    configs: list[triton.Config],  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n    grids: list[TritonGrid],\n    wrapper: Optional[PythonWrapperCodegen] = None,\n    original_fxnode_name: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    output = IndentedBuffer()\n\n    def _convert_to_sympy_expr(item: Union[int, sympy.Expr]) -> sympy.Expr:\n        return item if isinstance(item, sympy.Expr) else sympy.Integer(item)\n\n    def determine_grid(\n        grid: TritonGrid,\n        example_grid: Optional[TritonGrid] = None,\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        This function return a tuple of two values: the first one is for the real grid\n        which is used in the generated code; the second one is an example grid with\n        concreate values which is used in the autotune block to run the generated\n        kernels at compile time.\n        \"\"\"\n        if wrapper is None or callable(grid):\n            # return as-is when used in eager mode or when grid is callable\n            return grid, grid\n        # Grid contains ints/Expr, so utilize wrapper's expr printer for codegen\n        sympy_grid = tuple(_convert_to_sympy_expr(g) for g in grid)\n        if not example_grid:\n            example_grid = sympy_grid\n        return (\n            wrapper.codegen_python_shape_tuple(sympy_grid),\n            (\n                wrapper.codegen_python_shape_tuple(\n                    tuple(\n                        wrapper.generate_example_arg_value(g, type(g))\n                        for g in example_grid  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n                    )\n                )\n                if config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time\n                else None\n            ),\n        )\n\n    def writeline(line: str, example_grid: Optional[str] = None):\n        output.writeline(line)\n        if (\n            wrapper\n            and config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time\n            and name not in wrapper.kernel_autotune_names\n        ):\n            wrapper.kernel_autotune_calls.writeline(example_grid or line)\n\n    fn_name = f\"grid_wrapper_for_{name}\"\n    writeline(f\"def {fn_name}(meta):\")\n    kernel_autotune_calls_indent = (\n        wrapper.kernel_autotune_calls.indent()\n        if wrapper and config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time\n        else contextlib.nullcontext()\n    )\n    with output.indent(), kernel_autotune_calls_indent:\n        if (\n            config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time\n            and original_fxnode_name\n            and V.graph.autotuning_grids\n            and original_fxnode_name in V.graph.autotuning_grids\n        ):\n            example_grids = V.graph.autotuning_grids[original_fxnode_name]\n        else:\n            example_grids = [None] * len(grids)\n        if len(grids) == 1:\n            grid, example_grid = determine_grid(grids[0], example_grids[0])\n            writeline(f\"return {grid}\", f\"return {example_grid}\")\n        else:\n            assert len(grids) > 1\n            assert len(grids) == len(configs)\n            seen: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n            # sort the configs from the largest # of kwargs to the smallest to\n            # emit the grids in the order of (approximately) decreasing specificity\n            # TODO(aakhundov): the sorting below is generally not sufficient, so\n            # maybe we'll need to restrict the supported cases to identical kwarg\n            # names in all autotuning configs.\n            for grid, c, example_grid in sorted(\n                zip(grids, configs, example_grids),\n                key=lambda x: len(x[1].kwargs),\n                reverse=True,\n            ):\n                guardslist = []\n                if c.kwargs:\n                    # Remove AMD specific kwargs.\n                    for kwarg in c.kwargs:\n                        if kwarg not in [\n                            \"matrix_instr_nonkdim\",\n                            \"waves_per_eu\",\n                            \"kpack\",\n                        ]:\n                            guardslist.append(f\"meta['{kwarg}'] == {c.kwargs[kwarg]}\")\n                if guardslist:\n                    guards = \" and \".join(guardslist)\n                else:\n                    guards = \"True\"  # for configs with empty kwargs\n                grid, example_grid = determine_grid(grid, example_grid)\n                statement = f\"if {guards}: return {grid}\"\n                if statement in seen:\n                    continue\n                seen.add(statement)\n                writeline(statement, f\"if {guards}: return {example_grid}\")\n\n    return fn_name, output.getvalue()\n\n\ndef user_defined_triton_kernel_transitive_closure_source_code(kernel) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Given a triton kernel function pointer collect the transitive closure of\n    its dependencies\n    \"\"\"\n    compile_wrapper = IndentedBuffer()\n    compile_wrapper.splice(kernel.src, strip=True)\n\n    # Also include any possible kernel being called indirectly\n    import triton\n    from triton import JITFunction  # type: ignore[name-defined, attr-defined]\n    from triton.language import constexpr  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n\n    # global constexpr vars handled above\n    symbols_included = OrderedSet([kernel.__name__])\n\n    def traverse(cur_kernel):\n        # here we extract the unqualified names (i.e., not attributes and\n        # without prepended module name) loaded in the kernel code, which\n        # are matched with the co_names and __globals__ below to codegen\n        # the respective imports necessary for the kernel compilation\n        unqualified_loads = OrderedSet(\n            inst.argval\n            for inst in dis.Bytecode(cur_kernel.fn)\n            if inst.opname == \"LOAD_GLOBAL\"\n        )\n        global_annotations = cur_kernel.fn.__globals__.get(\"__annotations__\", {})\n        for symbol_name in cur_kernel.fn.__code__.co_names:\n            if symbol_name in symbols_included:\n                continue\n            if symbol_name in cur_kernel.fn.__globals__:\n                symbol = cur_kernel.fn.__globals__[symbol_name]\n                if isinstance(symbol, JITFunction):\n                    compile_wrapper.newline()\n                    compile_wrapper.writeline(\"@triton.jit\")\n                    compile_wrapper.splice(symbol.src, strip=True)\n                    symbols_included.add(symbol_name)\n                    traverse(symbol)\n                elif hasattr(triton, \"constexpr_function\") and isinstance(\n                    symbol, triton.runtime.jit.ConstexprFunction\n                ):\n                    compile_wrapper.newline()\n                    compile_wrapper.writeline(\"@triton.constexpr_function\")\n                    compile_wrapper.splice(symbol.src, strip=True)\n                    symbols_included.add(symbol_name)\n                    traverse(symbol)\n                elif isinstance(symbol, (int, str, bool, constexpr)):\n                    compile_wrapper.newline()\n                    if isinstance(symbol, constexpr):\n                        symbol_str = f\"tl.constexpr({symbol.value!r})\"\n                    else:\n                        symbol_str = f\"{symbol!r}\"\n                    if annotation := global_annotations.get(symbol_name):\n                        if isinstance(annotation, type):\n                            annotation_code = (\n                                f\": {annotation.__module__}.{annotation.__name__}\"\n                            )\n                        else:\n                            annotation_code = f\": {annotation!r}\"\n                        compile_wrapper.writeline(\n                            f\"{symbol_name}{annotation_code} = {symbol_str}\"\n                        )\n                    else:\n                        compile_wrapper.writeline(f\"{symbol_name} = {symbol_str}\")\n                    symbols_included.add(symbol_name)\n                elif (\n                    symbol_name in unqualified_loads\n                    and symbol_name != \"tl\"  # already imported\n                    and hasattr(symbol, \"__module__\")\n                    # only codegen imports from triton; JITFunctions\n                    # imported from other modules will be codegened\n                    # in the separate branch above\n                    and symbol.__module__.startswith(\"triton\")\n                ):\n                    # a global symbol imported from triton is referenced\n                    # without module qualification (i.e., `store` instead\n                    # of `tl.store`): need to codegen an import\n                    compile_wrapper.writeline(\n                        f\"from {symbol.__module__} import {symbol.__name__} as {symbol_name}\"\n                    )\n                    symbols_included.add(symbol_name)\n\n    traverse(kernel)\n    return compile_wrapper.getvalue()\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass SymbolicCallArg:\n    inner: sympy.Symbol\n    # the original symbolic expression represented by inner\n    inner_expr: sympy.Expr\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return str(self.inner)\n\n\nclass MemoryPlanningState:\n    def __init__(self):\n        super().__init__()\n        self.reuse_pool: dict[ReuseKey, list[FreeIfNotReusedLine]] = (\n            collections.defaultdict(list)\n        )\n        self.total_allocated_buffer_size: int = 0\n\n    def __contains__(self, key: ReuseKey) -> bool:\n        return bool(self.reuse_pool.get(key, None))\n\n    def pop(self, key: ReuseKey) -> FreeIfNotReusedLine:\n        item = self.reuse_pool[key].pop()\n        assert not item.is_reused\n        return item\n\n    def push(self, key: ReuseKey, item: FreeIfNotReusedLine) -> None:\n        assert not item.is_reused\n        self.reuse_pool[key].append(item)\n\n\nclass WrapperLine:\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        raise NotImplementedError(\"FX codegen not yet supported for type {type(self)}\")\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass EnterSubgraphLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    graph: GraphLowering\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        self.wrapper.push_computed_sizes(self.wrapper.computed_sizes)\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        self.wrapper.push_codegened_graph(self.graph)\n        code.do_indent()\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_enter_subgraph\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass CommentLine(WrapperLine):\n    line: LineContext\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        code.writeline(self.line)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def codegen_fx(converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_comment\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ExitSubgraphLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        self.wrapper.computed_sizes = self.wrapper.pop_computed_sizes()\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        self.wrapper.pop_codegened_graph()\n        code.do_unindent()\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_exit_subgraph\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass EnterDeviceContextManagerLine(WrapperLine):\n    device_idx: int\n    last_seen_device_guard_index: Optional[int]\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        if V.graph.cpp_wrapper:\n            code.writeline(\"\\n\")\n            if V.graph.aot_mode:\n                # In AOT mode, we have a stream provided as a param. A stream is\n                # associated with a device, so we never expect the device to change.\n                # CUDAStreamGuard sets the stream and the device.\n                if self.last_seen_device_guard_index is None:\n                    code.writeline(\n                        f\"{V.graph.device_ops.cpp_aoti_stream_guard()} stream_guard(stream, this->device_idx_);\"\n                    )\n                else:\n                    assert self.last_seen_device_guard_index == self.device_idx, (\n                        \"AOTInductor only supports running on one CUDA device\"\n                    )\n            else:\n                if self.last_seen_device_guard_index is None:\n                    code.writeline(\n                        f\"{V.graph.device_ops.cpp_aoti_device_guard()} device_guard({self.device_idx});\"\n                    )\n                else:\n                    code.writeline(f\"device_guard.set_index({self.device_idx});\")\n        else:\n            # Note _DeviceGuard has less overhead than device, but only accepts\n            # integers\n            code.writeline(f\"with {V.graph.device_ops.device_guard(self.device_idx)}:\")\n            code.do_indent()\n            code.writeline(V.graph.device_ops.set_device(self.device_idx))\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_enter_device_context_manager\n\n\nclass ExitDeviceContextManagerLine(WrapperLine):\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        if not V.graph.cpp_wrapper:\n            code.do_unindent()\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_exit_device_context_manager\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ExternKernelAllocLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    node: ir.ExternKernelAlloc\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        node = self.node\n        args = [*node.codegen_args(), *node.codegen_kwargs()]\n        self.wrapper._generate_extern_kernel_alloc_helper(self.node, args)\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_extern_kernel_alloc\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ExternKernelOutLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    node: ir.ExternKernelOut\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        node = self.node\n        args = [*node.codegen_args(), *node.codegen_kwargs(skip_out=True)]\n        kernel_name = node.get_kernel_name()\n        if (\n            V.graph.cpp_wrapper\n            and node.cpp_kernel_name == \"torch::inductor::_mm_plus_mm\"\n        ):\n            # For https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/128474\n            kernel_name = \"aoti_torch__mm_plus_mm_out\"\n        else:\n            kernel_name = node.get_kernel_name()\n        device = d.type if (d := node.get_device()) else V.graph.device_type\n        provenance_debug_handle: Optional[int] = None\n        # set provenance tracing kernel mapping for ExternKernel types\n        if config.trace.provenance_tracking_level != 0:\n            provenance_debug_handle = set_kernel_post_grad_provenance_tracing(\n                node, kernel_name, is_extern=True\n            )\n        self.wrapper._generate_extern_kernel_out_helper(\n            kernel_name,\n            node.codegen_reference(),\n            node.output_view.codegen_reference() if node.output_view else None,\n            args,\n            device,\n            provenance_debug_handle,\n        )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_extern_kernel_out\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass FreeLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    node: Union[BufferLike, ir.TorchBindObject]\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        assert self.node.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        code.writeline(self.wrapper.make_buffer_free(self.node))\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_free\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass KernelCallLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    kernel_name: str\n    call_args: tuple[Any, ...]\n    raw_keys: tuple[Any, ...]\n    raw_args: tuple[Any, ...]\n    arg_types: list[str]\n    triton: bool\n    triton_meta: dict[str, Any]\n    device: torch.device\n    graph_name: str\n    original_fxnode_name: str\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        self.wrapper._generate_kernel_call_helper(\n            self.kernel_name,\n            self.call_args,\n            triton=self.triton,\n            arg_types=self.arg_types,\n            raw_keys=self.raw_keys,\n            raw_args=self.raw_args,\n            triton_meta=self.triton_meta,\n            device=self.device,\n            graph_name=self.graph_name,\n            original_fxnode_name=self.original_fxnode_name,\n        )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_kernel_call\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass KernelDefinitionLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    kernel_name: str\n    kernel_body: str\n    metadata: Optional[str] = None\n    gpu: bool = True\n    cpp_definition: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        self.wrapper._define_kernel_helper(\n            self.kernel_name,\n            self.kernel_body,\n            metadata=self.metadata,\n            gpu=self.gpu,\n            cpp_definition=self.cpp_definition,\n        )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_kernel_definition\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass MemoryPlanningLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n\n    def plan(self, state: MemoryPlanningState) -> MemoryPlanningLine:\n        \"\"\"First pass to find reuse\"\"\"\n        return self\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Second pass to output code\"\"\"\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Emits a string representation that fits on one line.\n        \"\"\"\n        args: list[str] = []\n        for field in dataclasses.fields(self):\n            if field.name == \"wrapper\":\n                continue\n            val = getattr(self, field.name)\n            args.append(\n                f\"{field.name}={val.get_name() if field.type is ir.Buffer else val}\"\n            )\n        return f\"{type(self).__name__}({', '.join(args)})\"\n\n\nclass EfficientPeakEstimate:\n    def __init__(self):\n        from ..memory import estimate_peak_memory, get_freeable_input_buf\n\n        scheduler_nodes = V.graph.scheduler.nodes\n        graph_inputs = OrderedSet(V.graph.graph_inputs.keys())\n        graph_outputs = OrderedSet(V.graph.get_output_names())\n        names_to_freeable_bufs = get_freeable_input_buf(scheduler_nodes, graph_inputs)\n        self.overall_peak_memory, peak_by_scheduler_node = estimate_peak_memory(\n            scheduler_nodes,\n            names_to_freeable_bufs,\n            graph_outputs,\n        )\n\n        from .segmented_tree import SegmentedTree\n\n        self.segmented_tree = SegmentedTree(\n            peak_by_scheduler_node, operator.add, max, 0\n        )\n\n    def _get_size(self, node: BufferLike) -> int:\n        return V.graph.sizevars.size_hint(\n            V.graph.get_allocation_storage_size(node), fallback=0\n        ) * get_dtype_size(node.get_dtype())\n\n    def peak_between(self, line_a: FreeIfNotReusedLine, line_b: AllocateLine):\n        return self.segmented_tree.summarize_range(\n            line_a.scheduler_node_index + 1, line_b.scheduler_node_index - 1\n        )\n\n    def update_peak_between(self, line_a: FreeIfNotReusedLine, line_b: AllocateLine):\n        if line_a.scheduler_node_index + 1 == line_b.scheduler_node_index:\n            return\n        self.segmented_tree.update_range(\n            line_a.scheduler_node_index + 1,\n            line_b.scheduler_node_index - 1,\n            self._get_size(line_b.node),\n        )\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass AllocateLine(MemoryPlanningLine):\n    node: BufferLike\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        assert V.graph.scheduler.current_node is not None\n        self.scheduler_node_index = V.graph.scheduler.nodes.index(\n            V.graph.scheduler.current_node\n        )\n\n    def should_reuse_buffer(self, free_line: FreeIfNotReusedLine, size: int) -> bool:\n        if free_line.scheduler_node_index + 1 == self.scheduler_node_index:\n            return True\n        overall_peak_memory = self.wrapper.estimate_peak.overall_peak_memory\n        peak_memory_in_range = self.wrapper.estimate_peak.peak_between(free_line, self)\n        new_peak_memory = size + peak_memory_in_range\n        return new_peak_memory <= overall_peak_memory\n\n    def plan(self, state: MemoryPlanningState) -> MemoryPlanningLine:\n        if self.node.get_name() in V.graph.removed_buffers:\n            return NullLine(self.wrapper)\n\n        # try to reuse a recently freed buffer\n        key = buffer_reuse_key(self.node)\n        if config.allow_buffer_reuse and key in state:\n            free_line = state.pop(key)\n            size = V.graph.sizevars.size_hint(\n                V.graph.get_allocation_storage_size(self.node), fallback=0\n            ) * get_dtype_size(self.node.get_dtype())\n            if self.should_reuse_buffer(free_line, size):\n                free_line.is_reused = True\n                self.wrapper.estimate_peak.update_peak_between(free_line, self)\n                return ReuseLine(self.wrapper, free_line.node, self.node)\n            else:\n                state.push(key, free_line)\n                return self\n\n        if self.node.get_device_or_error().type == \"cpu\":\n            static_shape = self.wrapper.static_shape_for_buffer_or_none(self.node)\n            if static_shape is not None:\n                state.total_allocated_buffer_size += int(\n                    functools.reduce(operator.mul, static_shape, 1)\n                )\n\n        return self\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        assert self.node.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        line = self.wrapper.make_buffer_allocation(self.node)\n        code.writeline(line)\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_allocate\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass FreeIfNotReusedLine(MemoryPlanningLine):\n    node: BufferLike\n    is_reused: bool = False\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        assert V.graph.scheduler.current_node is not None\n        self.scheduler_node_index = V.graph.scheduler.nodes.index(\n            V.graph.scheduler.current_node\n        )\n\n    def plan(self, state: MemoryPlanningState) -> MemoryPlanningLine:\n        if len(self.node.get_inputs_that_alias_output()) > 0:\n            return self\n        if isinstance(self.node.layout, ir.MultiOutputLayout):\n            return self\n        assert not self.is_reused\n        if self.node.get_name() in V.graph.removed_buffers:\n            return NullLine(self.wrapper)\n        if config.allow_buffer_reuse:\n            state.push(buffer_reuse_key(self.node), self)\n        return self\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        assert self.node.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        if not self.is_reused:\n            code.writeline(self.wrapper.make_buffer_free(self.node))\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_free_if_not_reused\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ReinterpretLine(MemoryPlanningLine):\n    node: BufferLike\n    reused_as: BufferLike\n    layout: ir.Layout\n\n    def plan(self, state: MemoryPlanningState) -> MemoryPlanningLine:\n        return self\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        assert isinstance(self.layout, ir.NonOwningLayout)\n        assert isinstance(self.layout.view, ir.ReinterpretView)\n        self.wrapper.codegen_deferred_allocation(\n            self.reused_as.get_name(), self.layout.view\n        )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_reinterpret\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ReuseLine(MemoryPlanningLine):\n    node: BufferLike\n    reused_as: BufferLike\n    delete_old: bool = True\n\n    def plan(self, state: MemoryPlanningState) -> MemoryPlanningLine:\n        if self.node.get_name() in V.graph.removed_buffers:\n            assert self.reused_as.get_name() in V.graph.removed_buffers\n            return NullLine(self.wrapper)\n        assert self.reused_as.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        return self\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        assert self.node.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        assert self.reused_as.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        code.writeline(\n            self.wrapper.make_buffer_reuse(self.node, self.reused_as, self.delete_old)\n        )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_reuse\n\n\nclass NullLine(MemoryPlanningLine):\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_null\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass CommBufferLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen  # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821\n    node: ir.Buffer\n\n    @property\n    def size(self) -> int:\n        from torch._inductor.utils import is_symbolic\n\n        numel = self.node.get_numel()\n        dtype = self.node.get_dtype()\n        if is_symbolic(numel):\n            raise AssertionError(\n                f\"The size of a comm buffer can't be symbolic: {self.node}\"\n            )\n        return int(numel) * dtype.itemsize\n\n    @property\n    def comm_buffer_type(self) -> ir.CommBufferType:\n        layout = self.node.get_output_spec()\n        assert isinstance(layout, ir.CommBufferLayout)\n        return layout.comm_buffer_type\n\n    @property\n    def group_name(self) -> str:\n        layout = self.node.get_output_spec()\n        assert isinstance(layout, ir.CommBufferLayout)\n        return layout.group_name\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass CommBufferAllocateLine(CommBufferLine):\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        assert self.node.get_name() not in V.graph.removed_buffers\n        name = self.node.get_name()\n        device = self.node.get_device()\n        dtype = self.node.get_dtype()\n        shape = tuple(self.node.get_size())\n        stride = tuple(self.node.get_stride())\n        code.writeline(\n            self.make_allocation_line(\n                self.comm_buffer_type,\n                self.group_name,\n                self.wrapper,\n                name,\n                device,\n                dtype,\n                shape,\n                stride,\n            )\n        )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def make_allocation_line(\n        comm_buffer_type, group_name, wrapper, name, device, dtype, shape, stride\n    ):\n        if comm_buffer_type == ir.CommBufferType.SYMM_MEM:\n            return (\n                f\"{name} = empty_strided_p2p(\"\n                f\"{wrapper.codegen_shape_tuple(shape)}, \"\n                f\"{wrapper.codegen_shape_tuple(stride)}, \"\n                f\"{dtype}, \"\n                f'torch.device(\"cuda:{device.index}\"), '\n                f'group_name=\"{group_name}\", '\n                f\"alloc_id={random.randint(0, 2**64 - 1)})\"\n            )\n        else:\n            raise NotImplementedError(\n                f\"Unsupported comm buffer type: {comm_buffer_type}\"\n            )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_comm_buffer_allocate\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass CommBufferFreeLine(CommBufferLine):\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        line = self.wrapper.make_buffer_free(self.node)\n        code.writeline(f\"{line} # {self.comm_buffer_type.value} buffer free\")\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_comm_buffer_free\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass MultiOutputLine(WrapperLine):\n    \"\"\"\n    Given a MultiOutputLayout buffer, indexes actual buffer(s) from the result.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    result_name: str\n    arg_name: str\n    indices: Sequence[Any]\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        def codegen_list_tuple_access(basename, indices):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]\n            if len(indices) > 0:\n                itype, i = indices[0]\n                if issubclass(itype, list):\n                    return codegen_list_tuple_access(f\"{basename}[{i}]\", indices[1:])\n                elif issubclass(itype, tuple):\n                    # cpp wrapper code needs to use std::get<> to access a tuple\n                    tuple_access = self.wrapper.codegen_tuple_access(\n                        basename, self.result_name, str(i)\n                    )\n                    return codegen_list_tuple_access(tuple_access, indices[1:])\n                elif issubclass(itype, dict):\n                    return codegen_list_tuple_access(f\"{basename}['{i}']\", indices[1:])\n                else:\n                    raise AssertionError(\"non supported index type: \", itype)\n            else:\n                return basename\n\n        value = codegen_list_tuple_access(self.arg_name, self.indices)\n        code.writeline(\n            f\"{self.wrapper.declare}{self.result_name} = {value}{self.wrapper.ending}\"\n        )\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_multi_output\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass SymbolicCallArgLine(WrapperLine):\n    wrapper: PythonWrapperCodegen\n    arg: SymbolicCallArg\n    graph: GraphLowering\n\n    def codegen(self, code: IndentedBuffer) -> None:\n        self.wrapper._generate_symbolic_call_arg_helper(self.arg, self.graph)\n\n    def codegen_fx(self, converter: FxConverter) -> FxConversionFunc:\n        return converter._generate_symbolic_call_arg\n\n\nBufferName = str\nLine = Union[MemoryPlanningLine, LineContext]\n\n\nclass PythonWrapperCodegen(CodeGen):\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate outer wrapper in Python that calls the kernels.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    supports_caching = True  # Whether the output code is cacheable.\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        super().__init__()\n        self._names_iter: Iterator[int] = count()\n        self.args_to_buffers: dict[\n            str, Union[None, ir.TensorBox, ir.Buffer, ir.TorchBindObject]\n        ] = {}\n        self.imports = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.header = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.prefix = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.suffix = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.kernel_declarations = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.wrapper_call = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.kernel_autotune_defs = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.kernel_autotune_calls = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.subgraph_definitions = IndentedBuffer()\n        self.kernel_autotune_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n        # Map key is the kernel argument name; value is a tuple of the resulting example\n        # tensor name with the kernel where that tensor was most recently used.\n        self.kernel_autotune_example_args: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {}\n        self.kernel_autotune_tmp_arg_idx: int = 0\n        # If the generated source code is exactly the same, reuse the\n        # pre-existing kernel for it\n        self.src_to_kernel: dict[str, str] = {}\n        self.kernel_numel_expr: OrderedSet[tuple[str, GraphLowering]] = OrderedSet()\n        self.lines: list[Line] = []\n        self.declare = \"\"\n        self.declare_maybe_reference = \"\"\n        self.ending = \"\"\n        self.comment = \"#\"\n        self.none_str = \"None\"\n        self.move_begin = \"std::move(\" if V.graph.cpp_wrapper else \"\"\n        self.move_end = \")\" if V.graph.cpp_wrapper else \"\"\n        self.last_seen_device_guard_index: Optional[int] = None\n        self.supports_intermediate_hooks = True\n        self.user_defined_kernel_cache: dict[tuple[Any, ...], tuple[str, Any]] = {}\n        self.unbacked_symbol_decls: OrderedSet[str] = (\n            OrderedSet()\n        )  # str of sympy.Symbol\n        self.computed_sizes: OrderedSet[sympy.Symbol] = OrderedSet()\n        self.launcher_fn_name = None\n        # This function can be overridden to change the launcher name\n        self.set_launcher_fn_name()\n\n        # this is used for tracking which GraphLowering instance---parent graph\n        # or (nested) subgraph---is currently codegened; the primary use case is\n        # including the graph instance into a cache key to avoid cross-graph\n        # caching during lowering of nested subgraphs\n        self.codegened_graph_stack = []\n        self.computed_sizes_stack = []\n\n        self.write_header()\n\n        if not is_codegen_graph_partition_subgraph(self):\n            # See [Note: Removed Graph Partition Arguments]\n            self.write_prefix()\n\n        self.write_kernel_autotune_defs_header()\n\n        if not V.graph.aot_mode:\n            for name, hashed in V.graph.constant_reprs.items():\n                # include a hash so our code cache puts different constants into different files\n                self.write_constant(name, hashed)\n\n        self.allocated = OrderedSet[BufferName]()\n        self.freed = OrderedSet[BufferName]()\n\n        # maps from reusing buffer to reused buffer\n        self.reuses: dict[BufferName, BufferName] = {}\n\n        self.write_get_raw_stream = functools.lru_cache(None)(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            self.write_get_raw_stream\n        )\n\n        @functools.cache\n        def add_import_once(line: str) -> None:\n            self.imports.writeline(line)\n            if config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time:\n                self.kernel_autotune_calls.writeline(line)\n\n        self.add_import_once = add_import_once\n        self._metas: dict[str, str] = {}\n        self._meta_vars: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n        self.multi_kernel_state = MultiKernelState()\n        self.already_codegened_subgraphs: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n        self.allocated_workspaces: dict[str, Any] = {}\n\n        # intermediate tensor value printing utility\n        self.debug_printer = DebugPrinterManager(\n            debug_printer_level=config.aot_inductor.debug_intermediate_value_printer,\n            use_array_ref=config.aot_inductor.allow_stack_allocation,\n        )\n\n        # Additional files that are dependent to the wrapper (ex. cubin files)\n        self.additional_files = []\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def create(\n        is_subgraph: bool,\n        subgraph_name: Optional[str],\n        parent_wrapper: Optional[PythonWrapperCodegen],\n        partition_signatures: Optional[ir.GraphPartitionSignature] = None,\n    ):\n        if is_subgraph:\n            assert subgraph_name is not None\n            assert parent_wrapper is not None\n            return SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen(\n                subgraph_name, parent_wrapper, partition_signatures\n            )\n        return PythonWrapperCodegen()\n\n    def set_launcher_fn_name(self) -> None:\n        self.launcher_fn_name = \"call\"\n\n    def write_constant(self, name: str, hashed: str) -> None:\n        self.header.writeline(f\"{name} = None  # {hashed}\")\n\n    def write_header(self) -> None:\n        context = torch._guards.TracingContext.try_get()\n        aot_config_comment = \"\"\n        if context is not None and context.aot_graph_name is not None:\n            aot_config_comment = f\"# AOT ID: {context.aot_graph_name}\"\n        inductor_debug_utils = \"\"\n        if int(config.aot_inductor.debug_intermediate_value_printer) > 0:\n            inductor_debug_utils = \"from torch._inductor.codegen.debug_utils import _print_debugging_tensor_value_info\"\n        elif torch._inductor.config.test_configs.track_memory_lifecycle:\n            inductor_debug_utils = \"from torch._inductor.runtime.debug_utils import tracked_empty_strided\\n\"\n\n        self.imports.splice(\n            f\"\"\"\n                {aot_config_comment}\n                from ctypes import c_void_p, c_long, c_int\n                import torch\n                import math\n                import random\n                import os\n                import tempfile\n                from math import inf, nan\n                from cmath import nanj\n                from torch._inductor.hooks import run_intermediate_hooks\n                from torch._inductor.utils import maybe_profile\n                from torch._inductor.codegen.memory_planning import _align as
